prevalence.value: rare; focal sylvatic transmission with periodic outbreaks
prevalence.population: Brazilian population (risk highest in endemic/transition areas)
prevalence.trend: episodic outbreaks; risk influenced by vaccination coverage and epizootics in primates

burden.dalyPerCapita: low nationally; high severity in cases

riskFactors: lack of vaccination in risk areas
delayed supportive care for severe cases
vulnerablePopulations: populations near forest fragments in transition zones
epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: Clinical suspicion with jaundice/hemorrhage; confirmation by RT-PCR/serology; surveillance triggered by epizootics and human cases

publicHealthSystem.screeningCoverage: no screening; event-based surveillance and outbreak investigation
publicHealthSystem.controlRate: Prevention relies on vaccination; supportive hospital care for severe disease

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: SUS surveillance integrates epizootic monitoring in primates and lab confirmation in reference labs
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: No specific antiviral; supportive ICU care for fulminant hepatitis/bleeding; vaccination for prevention per PNI
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: Maintaining high vaccination coverage in expansion areas and rapid response to epizootics
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: Strong national immunization program (PNI) with effective vaccine and established outbreak response protocols
